title: Expected Value of Max and Min

source.file: content/A Practical Guide To Quantitative Finance Interviews Book by Xinfeng Zhou (chapters)/chapter-04-probability-theory.md

setup.narrative: X_1, X_2, ..., X_n are i.i.d. Uniform[0, 1] random variables.
rules: Each X_i has CDF F(x) = x and PDF f(x) = 1 on [0, 1].
setup.question: Find the CDF, PDF, and expected value of both Z_n and Y_n.

learningObjectives: Apply the order-statistic CDF formulas (F^n for max, 1 - (1-F)^n for min).
Integrate x * f(x) to compute expectations.

stages.label: CDF of the max
stages.prompt: Z_n = max(X_1, ..., X_n) <= x iff ALL X_i <= x. P(all X_i <= x) = ?

options.text: F(x)^n = x^n  (since each X_i has CDF F(x) = x).

stages.explanation: All n samples must be <= x. By independence, this is the product of n identical CDFs: x^n.

stages.label: PDF and E[Z_n]
stages.prompt: f_Z(x) = d/dx[x^n] = n * x^(n-1).  E[Z_n] = integral from 0 to 1 of x * n * x^(n-1) dx = ?
stages.explanation: Integrand is n * x^n. Antiderivative: x^(n+1) / (n+1) * n = n * x^(n+1) / (n+1). Evaluated 0 to 1: n / (n+1).

stages.label: CDF of the min
stages.prompt: Y_n > x iff ALL X_i > x. So F_Y(x) = 1 - P(all X_i > x). What's F_Y?
stages.explanation: P(all > x) = (1 - x)^n. So F_Y(x) = 1 - (1 - x)^n. PDF by differentiation: f_Y(x) = n(1 - x)^(n-1).

stages.label: Compute E[Y_n]
stages.prompt: E[Y_n] = integral from 0 to 1 of x * n * (1 - x)^(n-1) dx. Substitute u = 1 - x and integrate. (Or recognize symmetry: by symmetry of the uniform distribution about 1/2, E[Y_n] = 1 - E[Z_n] = 1 - n/(n+1) = 1/(n+1).)
stages.explanation: By symmetry: if X uniform on [0, 1] then 1 - X is also uniform on [0, 1]. So min(X) and 1 - max(1 - X) match. E[min] = 1 - E[max] = 1 - n/(n+1) = 1/(n+1).

stages.label: Commit
stages.explanation: n/(n+1) and 1/(n+1) for n=10. Sanity check: E[Z_n] + E[Y_n] = 1 (by symmetry) -- holds here. As n grows, Z_n -> 1 and Y_n -> 0 (extremes spread out).

stages.label: Reflection
prompts: Compute Var(Z_n) for the uniform[0, 1] case. (Use E[Z_n^2] = integral x^2 * n * x^(n-1) dx.)
Generalize to uniform on [a, b]: what are E[Z_n] and E[Y_n]?
What's E[Z_n] for n i.i.d. exponentials with rate lambda?
